tool_name: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation)

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Material & Equipment Cost
inputs.hint: Cost in today's money (base date). Include bulk materials, major equipment, vendor supply.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Labour Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Base Estimate — Subcontract Cost
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Material / Equipment Escalation Rate
inputs.hint: PPI (Producer Price Index) for relevant commodity — steel: check LME | cement: local | energy equipment: IHS Markit
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Labour / Wage Escalation Rate
inputs.hint: National wage inflation or collective agreement rate for project location
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Subcontract / Indirect Escalation Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Spend Profile (spend timing)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Nominal Discount Rate (WACC or project hurdle)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: General CPI Inflation Rate (project economy)
inputs.hint: National CPI forecast for project country — Turkey 2024: ~45% | EU: ~3% | USD zone: ~3%

inputs.name: Management Reserve (known-unknowns)
inputs.hint: PMBOK: separate from contingency — for scope changes beyond current baseline

formulas: Base_Total_Cost_USD = base_material_cost_usd + base_labour_cost_usd + base_subcontract_cost_usd   // [USD] Total base estimate
Effective_Duration_yrs = project_duration_years * spend_timing_factor   // [years] Weighted spend timing
Escalated_Material_USD = base_material_cost_usd * POWER(1 + material_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated material cost
Escalated_Labour_USD = base_labour_cost_usd * POWER(1 + labour_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated labour cost
Escalated_Subcontract_USD = base_subcontract_cost_usd * POWER(1 + subcontract_escalation_pct_yr / 100, Effective_Duration_yrs)   // [USD] Escalated subcontract
Total_Escalated_Base_USD = Escalated_Material_USD + Escalated_Labour_USD + Escalated_Subcontract_USD   // [USD] Total escalated estimate
Escalation_Amount_USD = Total_Escalated_Base_USD - Base_Total_Cost_USD   // [USD] Pure escalation delta
Escalation_Pct = (Escalation_Amount_USD / Base_Total_Cost_USD) * 100   // [%] Escalation as % of base
Budget_with_Contingency_USD = Total_Escalated_Base_USD + Contingency_USD   // [USD] Authorised project budget
Total_Project_Authorisation_USD = Budget_with_Contingency_USD + Management_Reserve_USD   // [USD] Total cost at completion (TCA)
Real_Discount_Rate_pct = ((1 + nominal_discount_rate_pct / 100) / (1 + general_inflation_rate_pct / 100) - 1) * 100   // [%] Real rate — Fisher Equation
NPV_Cost_Real_USD = Total_Project_Authorisation_USD / POWER(1 + Real_Discount_Rate_pct / 100, project_duration_years)   // [USD] Present value of total cost in real terms

base_cost_positive.error_msg: Total base cost must be positive. Both material and labour cannot be zero simultaneously.

duration_minimum.error_msg: Project duration minimum 0.1 years (approximately 5 weeks). Below this, escalation is negligible.

inflation_vs_discount.error_msg: Inflation rate equals or exceeds nominal discount rate — Real discount rate is zero or negative (Fisher Equation). This represents hyperinflationary conditions. NPV analysis in real terms becomes unreliable. Use nominal cash flows instead.

smart_warnings.message: NEGATIVE REAL DISCOUNT RATE: Inflation exceeds nominal WACC. Every deferred cost actually costs more in real terms — there is no financial benefit to delaying project expenditure. This typically signals a hyperinflationary environment. Nominal cash flow analysis is required instead of real.

HIGH ESCALATION (>20%): Cost escalation exceeds 20% of base estimate. This level typically represents a material budget risk. Project sanction budget must include full escalation provision — underfunded projects are the primary cause of cost overruns in capital projects. Escalate to project sponsor.

MATERIAL ESCALATION OUTPACING LABOUR >2×: Commodity price volatility is the dominant risk. Evaluate: (1) Fixed-price supply contracts with early procurement, (2) Material price escalation clauses in contracts, (3) Alternative materials or supplier diversification.

LOW CONTINGENCY (<5%): Insufficient contingency for any estimate class. AACE Class 3 (budget estimate) requires 10–20%. Underfunding contingency is the most common cause of cost overruns on capital projects.

NO MANAGEMENT RESERVE on multi-year project: Projects >3 years face significant scope uncertainty. Zero management reserve leaves no headroom for authorised scope additions. PMBOK recommends 5–10% management reserve separate from risk contingency.

ESCALATION EXCEEDS CONTINGENCY: Price escalation alone is larger than the entire contingency reserve. Contingency will be fully consumed by inflation before any technical risk event. Increase contingency or renegotiate fixed-price contracts to include escalation clauses.

fmea.effect: Total base cost must be positive. Both material and labour cannot be zero simultaneously.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Inflation rate equals or exceeds nominal discount rate — Real discount rate is zero or negative (Fisher Equation). This represents hyperinflationary conditions. NPV analysis in real terms becomes unreliable. Use nominal cash flows instead.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE REAL DISCOUNT RATE: Inflation exceeds nominal WACC. Every deferred cost actually costs more in real terms — there is no financial benefit to delaying project expenditure. This typically signals a hyperinflationary environment. Nominal cash flow analysis is required instead of real.

HIGH ESCALATION (>20%): Cost escalation exceeds 20% of base estimate. This level typically represents a material budget risk. Project sanction budget must include full escalation provision — underfunded projects are the primary cause of cost overruns in capital projects. Escalate to project sponsor.

MATERIAL ESCALATION OUTPACING LABOUR >2×: Commodity price volatility is the dominant risk. Evaluate: (1) Fixed-price supply contracts with early procurement, (2) Material price escalation clauses in contracts, (3) Alternative materials or supplier diversification.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: material_escalation_pct_yr > labour_escalation_pct_yr * 2

fmea.failureMode: LOW CONTINGENCY (<5%): Insufficient contingency for any estimate class. AACE Class 3 (budget estimate) requires 10–20%. Underfunding contingency is the most common cause of cost overruns on capital projects.

NO MANAGEMENT RESERVE on multi-year project: Projects >3 years face significant scope uncertainty. Zero management reserve leaves no headroom for authorised scope additions. PMBOK recommends 5–10% management reserve separate from risk contingency.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: project_duration_years > 3 && management_reserve_pct === 0

fmea.failureMode: ESCALATION EXCEEDS CONTINGENCY: Price escalation alone is larger than the entire contingency reserve. Contingency will be fully consumed by inflation before any technical risk event. Increase contingency or renegotiate fixed-price contracts to include escalation clauses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Escalation_Amount_USD > Contingency_USD
